profile.statement_to_voters:
I have always liked to give something back to my community whenever I can. Since joining the Liberal Democrats I have been encouraged to engage in local politics, campaigning to improve my local area and the lives of fellow residents.

Voter participation and engaged democracy are things I care for passionately and have worked to improve all of my adult life, particularly amongst younger voters. Whilst campaigning for Remain in the EU referendum, I spoke and took part in events motivating voters to sign up and make their voices heard and I believe that the public should be given the opportunity to have a vote on the final Brexit deal.

I am also passionate about the environment, as a member of several organisations and volunteering with conservation groups.

Born in Norwich, I live locally in Sewell, and work in the city centre.

I’m standing for Reform UK because I want to leave a better country and a better future for my children. This expectation that the next generation should inherit a better world from the previous generation has in the past been a given but now it is very much in doubt. The Conservatives, Labour and the Liberal Democrats have all presided over nearly three decades of managed decline of our economy, rapid change to our society and a sustained attack on our values.

Britain deserves much better and Reform UK are the only party offering real change and hope for a better future. The three legacy parties all believe in mass immigration, big government, high taxes, expensive energy and the woke agenda. Reform UK are the only party to reject this orthodoxy.

I have voted for the Conservatives in ten general elections but never again. Ideologically my position hasn’t changed over the years but the Tories have abandoned me and moved to the left, embracing globalist, corporatist values that I don’t align with. We need to put our country and its people first and that’s what Reform UK are proposing.

I have lived in Norwich for 23 years, I have raised my family here and started a small business here. Norfolk needs a fresh mandate in order to end decades of financial mismanagement. Tax payers deserve better services and better value for money from their local authority. Reform UK will run the council for the benefit of the people who pay for it rather than the people who work for it.

I’ve lived in Bowthorpe for over two decades and feel deeply for the community that I live in. I am standing for election in Bowthorpe as I want to offer a real, hopeful alternative to the traditional two-party system that has compassion and grassroots organisation at its core. I’ve spent my adult life working in health and social care, including at the NNUH, St Martins Housing Trust, New Routes Integration and as a frontline worker during the COVID-19 pandemic.

In 2026, life is hard for so many; expensive bills, inefficient services, inadequate mental health support, benefit cuts for the unwell and disabled, a crumbling NHS and no new social and council housing. If elected, I would like to support those struggling with the cost of living, tackle antisocial behaviour, advocate for residents regarding the future of the ward and protect and promote local green spaces, such as the Bowthorpe Marsh and Bowthorpe Southern Park.

A vote for me is a vote for a local girl who genuinely cares about those around her and who will always call out corruption, collusion and injustice.

profile.statement_to_voters:
As a fellow Peartree resident, I am deeply invested in delivering for our area and making it the best it can be for the residents of today and for future generations. With Local Government Reorganisation on the horizon, I want to stand up for our community by campaigning for the creation of a Welwyn Garden City Town Council. I want to continue tackling multiple housing issues, address parking problems alongside my fellow Peartree Councillors and address all the multitude of other issues residents bring to me.

In the last four years, I have played a substantial part in ensuring the Councils finances are on a secure footing as Cabinet member for Resources and more recently as the shadow for that position.

With your continued support, I will carry on working hard for Peartree Ward - listening, acting and standing up for our community.

Hi, I'm Tanya and I'm standing to be elected as your Green Party councillor for Peartree on Thursday 7th May.

As a Peartree resident, I put Green values into practice every day. I founded CultureWood CIC, a social enterprise where we are passionate about nature connection, running forest school programmes for local schools and for the wider community, working with people of all ages. I lead a local community garden group at King George V, where we are literally digging in to protect our environment, and are creating a community and green space where we can learn, share, grow food, build resilience and improve the park for nature.

I'm standing because our ward needs a councillor who will fight just as hard to protect our green spaces, tackle the cost of living crisis, and ensure the council acts on its climate promises.

Your choice in this election is really clear, it’s either the Lib Dems again, or you can vote for me and the Green Party on Thursday 7th May and help change Welwyn Hatfield Borough Council!

profile.statement_to_voters:
Having previous experience as a City Councillor, I have a track record of making tangible improvements to play areas, parks and green spaces and pushing the Labour-run council to improve its ambition, especially on climate change and biodiversity.

In my professional life, I am a hospital doctor and researcher, as well as a Trade Union representative defending NHS staff from unfair conditions. Since moving to West Chesterton, I have helped make sure pavements are safe and clear for pedestrians; worked to remove bollards which are obstructive to prams and wheelchairs. When previously a councillor, I spoke up for residents on many occasions at council, holding the Labour-run administration to account across key issues for our city; whether it be water, housing, the wider environment or ecology. I’m proud of my work as a community campaigner and hope that you will vote for more hard work in May.

I am a local resident in West Chesterton and have lived here my whole life. I am very proud of my home town and place of birth  and the leisure and green spaces that it provides. As an advocate for healthy living, I would love to see greater access to sports and leisure facilities across the city. As an avid cyclist, I understand that for many people in Cambridge including myself cycling is an integral part to our city wide identity.

For a number of years, during the summer months I have taken international students across the city on historic tours of the city. Recently, I have seen deterioration of public facilities within the city, including the closure of public toilets and inadequate maintenance of those that remain. I believe the city of Cambridge deserves better.

As a physiotherapy trainee and during my time at Addenbrookes I have an awareness of the importance of safeguarding our physical health. I believe the council has a responsibility to safeguard residents and to provide  them with sporting and leisure facilities,to aid wellbeing. I have seen a large increase in electric scooter related injuries recently and believe our city council isn’t doing enough to stop dangerous high-speed electric scooters on our commons and pavements.

As a psychology graduate, I am also passionate about mental health and believe the council should do more to foster a happy city by improving green areas and should end the local tennis court use fee that labour has introduced. I don’t believe taxation on physical activity is the correct way to support residents.

Contact Guy - mills.libdem@gmail.com

profile.statement_to_voters:
Born and lived in Leeds most of my life and my family have been in Leeds since the 1890’s.

Work & Education

Solicitor and Photographer, MA in International Studies specialising in the Middle East, Islamic Radicalism and the Cold War, Leeds University. LLM in Commercial Intellectual Property Law, Nottingham Trent

Why Reform UK

I have been active in local politics for over 30 years with both Labour and the Liberal Democrats. I have concluded that only a shift to Reform will save the country from failed Tory and Labour policies.

What Leeds needs

I have been advocating a mass transit system for Leeds based on a Monorail that will catapult the profile of the city worldwide also we need an upgrade to the Airport without delay for the future prosperity of the city.

Born and brought up in Leeds, Rosemary attended Lawnswood High School before going on o study at the universities of Bristol and Durham. On completing her teacher training she returned to Leeds to take up her first teaching post in Meanwood. After taking time out from teaching to bring up her three children, she retrained as a dyslexia specialist and has spent the last 21 years helping people of all ages overcome the literacy and numeracy difficulties associated with dyslexia and other forms of neurodiversity.

Her vision for Seacroft is for it to be a safe, welcoming community where everyone can lead their best life possible. She is particularly interested in housing, tackling antisocial behaviour and the management of open spaces."

Rosemary lives in Seacroft with her husband Peter and their cocker spaniel.

Rosemary can be contacted via email eastleedstories@gmail.com and is happy to answer any questions you may have.

Hello! My name is Nosheen and I’m the Green Party candidate for Killingbeck and Seacroft in the May 2026 elections.

I’ve lived in Seacroft for five years and work in the transport industry as a Controller.

I’ve always enjoyed living in Seacroft due to the numerous community intitiatives in the area, which I try to participate in as much as I’m able to.

I enjoy the massive community spirit that exists in the area and great warmth shown by many local residents to each other and towards me, making it a great pleasure to live here!

profile.statement_to_voters:
I'm Byran Driver, your local Green Party campaigner for Sutton & Mount Gould. I'm proud to be standing in the local elections here on Thursday 7th May. It's been great meeting so many of you and working to get things done for our community. If you're still deciding how to vote this May, here are a few reasons to consider voting for me.

I've lived in Plymouth all my life, but Sutton and Mount Gould made me who I am. My grandparents lived in Teats Hill. I grew up doing beach cleans at Coxside and even broke my arm in the local play park. As a young adult I moved to Sutton and Mount Gould to build my life and career.

After training in the Territorial Army, I went on to work as a carer for the elderly. I later moved into children's social care, where I work today. I know this area. I love this area. I want to help improve local services and help our community flourish as your councillor.

I got into politics because I could see how local government is failing our communities and wanted to roll up my sleeves to help make things better. I've already been working hard on the issues you care about - from reporting blocked drains and flooding to potholes, damaged play equipment, fly-tipping and litter. I'm also calling on the Council to save the much-loved Tothill Community Centre. As your councillor, I could do even more.

With 19 seats up for election on Plymouth City Council this May, whatever happens, Labour will still control the council. But by voting for me, you can elect a hard-working local councillor who will stand up for our area - not a Labour or Reform councillor who will have to follow the party line.

Labour's vote share is collapsing. Greens and Reform are surging in support. The latest data suggests the race in Sutton & Mount Gould is close between the Greens and Reform. It could come down to just a few votes. I can win but it'll be close with Reform, so please use your vote to elect a community champion who has a record of hard work locally.

Born in Plymouth, Served in the Royal Navy & Lived in Devonport for over 22 years. I want to be Devonport's voice on the local council.  I believe the council needs something different, something new. The council needs Reform UK.

profile.statement_to_voters:
I first came to Crookes & Crosspool a decade ago while studying at university, and fell in love with the area. I'm honoured to be your Labour & Co-operative councillor and to have the opportunity to serve our community.

I first stood for election because I wanted more action on housing, public transport, economic justice, and the climate justice. In that time I have introduced policies that have made our local economy fairer, and I have worked to ensure local services protect the most vulnerable.

After over a decade of austerity, I know there is much more to be done, and I hope you will continue to put your faith in me to stand up for social, economic, and climate justice at a local and national level.

Dylan Lewis-Creser is your candidate in Crookes and Crosspool for the local election on May 7th.

Dylan has lived in Crookes for 3 years, and is passionate about improving our neighbourhood's public transport, urban design, and reducing inequality in our area.

They said: "Like so many of us, I chose Sheffield as my home because of our community spirit, green spaces, and commitment to social justice.

We deserve a city council that works for all of us - voting for me means you get a councillor that understands and that will actively listen to our communities, and work hard to make change."

Ricia grew up by Crosspool’s crags with horses in fields lining lanes now absorbing Malin Bridge bottleneck-avoiding traffic, making it too dangerous to cycle, ride or walk along. 
 
 ”Motorists burn extra fuel taking circuitous routes I could safely cross to ballet school and church halls where, at 10, I saw Joe Cocker. I still support local music:  RiciaBHC Youtube shorts.” 
 
Post Notre Dame High School, Ricia graduated from Leeds University and taught at Shirecliffe College before going to Petroleum Development Oman where she became Ricia: Arabic for feather.” 
 
Then in Boston Mass. she gained an MA while working as a journalist, leading to Economist Newspaper Ltd assignments overseas making her very conscious of what draws and deters investment.  
 
“To pay for all its residents demand and politicians promise to win votes, Sheffield must be more enterprise-friendly to increase productivity per capita and ensure resources aren’t wasted. Cities need those who understand this at the helm. Lenders say no as risks of defaulting rise.” 
 
A local live music supporting landlady agrees: “Sheffield must be run like a good pub, keeping customers happy but turning a profit, otherwise there’s no money for staff.”

profile.statement_to_voters:
Representing the people of Eccles as your Labour Councillor over the past five years has been the privilege of my life. I'm proud to once again stand to represent the community I was raised in as your Labour candidate.

During this time, I have worked to deliver major regeneration for Eccles. I will continue to ensure residents' voices shape our plans as we develop a masterplan to transform our town, creating a vibrant centre, improving green spaces, and protecting our historic buildings.

We have already begun, with investment in the Town Hall and restoration of the Carnegie Library at the Gateway. I remain committed to making Eccles a better place for everyone.

I have also supported local community groups, especially those helping older residents, through fundraising and volunteering at events such as Eccles VIP activities and the Ellesmere Park Estate Supper Club.

My experience as an academic, trade union representative with GB and UCU, and trustee for local charities has strengthened my ability to stand up for local people.

Over the next four years, I will focus on a greener Eccles, safer communities, stronger local groups, tackling fly-tipping, and improving our roads.

I would be honoured to continue representing you and hope for your support.

Sara grew up in Eccles and still lives locally with her family. She is a mum and has worked in social housing for over 20 years, supporting people with housing, health and financial challenges.

She has always been actively involved in the community — as a Brownie Leader (Little Owl), a member of the Monton Green Primary School PTA, and as a School Governor for four years. These experiences have shaped her strong commitment to supporting children, families and local communities.

Sara is standing as a Green Party candidate because she cares about the area and wants to see Eccles thrive. With regeneration underway, she believes it is important that it benefits the people who already live here — with genuinely affordable homes, support for local businesses, and real opportunities for young people.

Through her work and her own experience, Sara understands how rising costs and financial pressures are affecting households, and how important it is that people have access to affordable housing and the support they need.

She also believes in creating healthier, greener neighbourhoods, with well-maintained, safe and welcoming parks and public spaces — including the Greenway and new green spaces created through regeneration — so that everyone can enjoy where they live. This includes taking concerns about anti-social behaviour seriously and working with local partners to help keep communities safe.

The Green Party focuses its limited resources on a small number of areas, so residents may not see Sara knocking on every door in Eccles this time. However, she is always happy to hear from local people and welcomes anyone getting in touch to share their views or concerns.

Sara believes local politics should focus on listening to residents, thinking long-term, and working together to build a fairer, healthier and more sustainable community.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Local teacher, Cllr Matthew Fido , is seeking re-election to continue as your local councillor.

I have been proud to serve the Moorland community in City Hall for the last 3 years. As a teacher at a local secondary school, I have worked closely with young people, their families, and the wider community, putting them at the forefront of all I do. I have collaborated with local groups and churches to assist families during the cost-of-living crisis, organising collections and distributing food parcels to families in need. Through committee work, I have contributed towards the council’s communication about cost-of-living support to families most in need.

I am an advocate for our local environment. Lincoln is fortunate to be blessed with many green spaces and waterways running through the city. It is important that everyone does their bit to look after the environment we live in so we can all be proud of our community. I regularly report issues around fly-tipping and dog fouling to keep our community looking pretty and feeling safe. This is on top of reporting potholes and broken streetlights to the County Council. Lincoln is an ever-growing city, and thus needs infrastructure investment to help keep growth sustainable. I am proud to have been part of the Conservative administration on the County Council that has delivered the Lincoln Eastern Bypass. Conservative councillors are also working together to address issues such as anti-social behavior and the impacts of the Western Growth Corridor development.

By re-electing me, Matthew Fido, in May, you will be getting a dynamic councillor representing our community in City Hall, making sure everyone’s voice is heard, not just another Labour councillor who nods through decisions like cancelling our Christmas Market.

I've lived in this electoral division for 8 years, so I understand local issues.

A Commando for 22 years rising to captain, then 13 years managing director in armed security, including bomb disposal.

I'm an expert in tackling waste, corruption and delivering value for money.

I will champion real improvement.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Tim is a local resident and has been an active campaigner in Fratton for the last few years,  with his ancestral routes tracing back to nearby Emsworth. 

Tim works as a software contractor and AI specialist. In his career, he has been involved in problem solving and delivery of solutions in complex research and engineering projects. His understanding of evidence based policy is needed to address the significant challenges we face.

After seeing worrying signs of environmental and social break down, Tim decided to get involved in politics and local campaigns. He has had a long interest in environmental issues, civil rights and membership of the EU. Tim currently specializes in air quality and sustainable transport issues. He has volunteered with Portsmouth Foodcycle and Fratton Big Local to help support the local community.

"We need to abandon old dogmatic solutions in favour of sustainable and accountable systems. Of particular concern in Portsmouth is the level of traffic, which causes parking problems, delays, pollution and climate change. I will fight for more investment in cycling, walking and public transport, as well as requiring development schemes to put these priorities at their centre. Green spaces need to be introduced around the city to improve well being. The city also needs to improve its recycling rates, which are among the worst in the country.

I am an air quality campaigner and regular blogger on the #LetPompeyBreathe campaign and I support efforts to remain in the EU."

Hello, I’m Jacob Short, I’m a local lad living in Fratton, I attended a local secondary school and went to Portsmouth College where I studied Law, Politics and Digitial Media/Journalism.

I have decided to stand to be a local voice on a national stage, without influence from national parties and to be able to represent the constituents of Portsmouth South without external pressures from party whips. No towing the party line.

Portsmouth is an amazing city with a wide range of diverse cultures and communities, with amazing local businesses and charities. But, we are also facing many issues that need to be addressed by a strong, active and committed local person.

Our local infrastructure is falling apart. Housing issues and homelessness is on the increase, our roads are crumbling with potholes everywhere and people cannot access the basics like a dentist or GP. Then there’s the shop lifting, anti social behaviour, joyriding across the city. The dumping of sewage into our seas is another strong issue being known as a “waterfront city”, why can’t we swim in our seas?

Vote for a local person who puts Portsmouth South first and foremost. Vote for change. Vote Portsmouth Independents.

Billy was born and brought up in Portsmouth. He lives in Fratton and works for an engineering firm on the Eastern Road. He has a large local family and knows the city well.

Billy, Cllr Dave Ashmore and Cllr Stuart Brown listened to local people when they asked for help with parking. Now there are Residents Parking Schemes across Fratton because of the Lib Dem councillors.

**Billy lives here and has a record of service and action in the city**

Billy cares about our area. He lives in Fratton and has a record of local action. Billy helped to get outside funding for the D-Day commemorations for the city.

He knows the area like the back of his hand. Billy has built up a positive reputation as
someone who helps and supports local people.

**Billy Ansell works hard for you**

Billy conducted the largest survey on local NHS and health care issues in Fratton. He is campaigning for more NHS dentists.

profile.statement_to_voters: Martyn has worked in education in various roles, including college principal and now as an education consultant. He joined the Labour Party because he believes in equality, justice and fairness. Martyn has been Labour Party Secretary and Chair and was a councillor for Bridgemary North. He wants to represent all residents of Bridgemary and fight for the distinctive nature of the community. He believes that Gosport has been wrecked by the Conservatives: local democracy has been damaged by weak leadership and a lack of vision. Only Labour has the vision and the ability to turn things around.

Roger, a retired teacher, has lived in the Meon Valley for most of his working life and in Swanmore for the past twenty years. He has represented Shirrell Heath, Shedfield and Curdridge on Winchester District Council for six years, and gained a reputation for being hard-working, and fighting to protect our rural communities. He is a regular participant in Speedwatch initiatives and was one of the organisers of the Stop the Quarry Campaign in Shedfield.

Resident in Meon Valley for over 20 years, Malcolm is an engineer with many years’ experience leading large, multi-national industrial projects. Malcolm values the special qualities of the natural environment of the Meon Valley and will always seek to protect them. Addressing the climate emergency is both a global and a local responsibility that will require strengthened communities, bringing back services and local businesses. 

As a current Winchester City Councillor, he has worked hard to speak up for our area including successfully combatting over-development in our villages, protecting our local rivers and waterways and improved accessibility for our residents. He is looking forward to achieve even more for the residents on Hampshire County Council.

Hampshire County Council provides local services like schools, roads and social services. Our area (Meon Valley) has had a Conservative County Councillor for more than 40 years. Many people think it’s time for a change.

Green Cllr Malcolm Wallace came second to the Conservatives in the Hampshire County Election last time. Since then, the Green vote has grown massively across the Meon Valley, including winning two seats on Winchester Council with 63% last year! If this year’s County by-election was based on how residents voted in last year’s local elections, then the GREENS WOULD WIN.

For a strong, independent voice who will make a real difference at Hampshire County, vote for Green Cllr Malcolm Wallace!

profile.statement_to_voters: John has lived and been involved in the Prettygate and Lexden area  since he first moved to Colchester as a child. Now retired, John worked in the NHS and in local government as and HR manager. When a member of Essex County Council, John was instrumental in making sure that Lexden Park was not turned into another housing estate and that County handed it over to Colchester Borough Council as a public open space. John has actively campaigned for residents of Prettygate over the last 10 years

Dear Resident

Let me take this opportunity to introduce myself and a little bit about why I am so passionate about representing the interests of Prettygate, where I work.

Having spent five years working alongside Cllr Sue Lissimore, Cllr Roger Buston and Cllr Leigh Tate across Prettgate and Lexden - now is the time to step and be its next city councillor.

With over twenty years experience working in Healthcare here in North Essex and specifically Colchester I have held management, clinical and voluntary positions with most of our NHS hospitals, private hospital providers, healthcare charities and local Ambulance service.

In 2012 I established a not-for-profit healthcare organisation providing free or affordable event medical provision and first aid training to hundreds of public, community and sporting events and charitable organisations across the East of England region and wider UK. Growing year on year to become the largest not-for-profit ambulance provider in East Anglia. The most rewarding highlight for me has been in supporting, mentoring and training multiple local young people to fulfill their dreams of becoming registered Nurses, Paramedics and other Allied Healthcare Professionals. I continued this passion for education as a part time Clinical Lecturer for Anaesthetic, paramedical and nursing students at a local university.

In 2023 I decided to swap nights shifts for more time with my son and young family. Since then I have been a postman and currently a post office worker here in The Commons, Prettygate.

I am married to Angela, a Ministry of Defence public servant of 28 years from Colchester and with a 9 year old son, community safety, cleanliness and improving the assessability of public services, recreational facilities and educational access Prettygate are also issues of pressing importance and very very close to my own heart.

After 5 years being proactive in Prettygate (lobbying, litterpicking, grafetti removal, sign cleaning and painting), I now want to be your next professional and engaging representative who delivers open, honest and fair representation for you all, both within Prettygate and Lexden working alongside my colleague and friend Cllr Sue Lissimore.

On Thursday May 7th 2026 please lend me your vote so I can do even more for our community, where I work and champion at every oppurtunity.

Kind regards

David

P.S Please do not hesitate to contact me for any further information, questions or issues you would like to raise/discuss.

t. 07791 434977  e. linghornbaker@yahoo.co.uk  
David Linghorn-Baker- Prettygate Conservative Candidate
